label: Fly.io
description: Deploy and manage edge applications on Fly.io's global platform using your API token.

form.title: Connect Fly.io
form.subtitle: Paste your Fly.io API token to let Anton manage and inspect your edge applications.

methods.label: API Token
methods.description: Paste a Fly.io personal access token. Generate one with `flyctl auth token` in your terminal or from the Access Tokens page in your Fly.io dashboard. Simplest and fastest setup.
methods.how_to:
## Get your Fly.io API Token

Fly.io uses personal access tokens (PATs) for API authentication. You can get one in two ways:

### Option A — flyctl CLI

1. Install `flyctl` if you haven't: https://fly.io/docs/hands-on/install-flyctl/
2. Log in with `flyctl auth login`.
3. Run `flyctl auth token` in your terminal.
4. Copy the printed token string.

### Option B — Web Dashboard

1. Open [https://fly.io/user/personal_access_tokens](https://fly.io/user/personal_access_tokens) (or go to **fly.io → Account → Access Tokens**).
2. Click **Create token**.
3. Give it a name like `Anton` and click **Create**.
4. Copy the token — it is shown only once.

### Paste into Anton

5. Paste the token into the **API Token** field below.
6. Optionally set your **Organization slug** if you want Anton to scope requests to a specific org (leave blank to use your personal org).

### Notes

- Tokens are shown once on creation. If you lose it, revoke the old token and create a new one from the Access Tokens page.
- To revoke, go to **Account → Access Tokens**, find the token, and click **Delete**.

fields.name: api_token
fields.label: API Token
fields.description: Your Fly.io personal access token. Run `flyctl auth token` or generate one at fly.io/user/personal_access_tokens.

fields.name: org_slug
fields.label: Organization Slug
fields.description: Optional. The slug of the Fly.io organization to scope requests to. Leave blank to use your personal organization.
